pages.raw_text:
The meeting of the trades council
last night was enlivened by an at
tack upon the Freemen's Labor Jour
nal and it's editor, e. w. Harbaugh, by
]. Gilbert. a warm supporter of
Hughes. socialist candidate for may
or Gilbert took exception to the
Labor Journals course in supporting
Byrne and Boyd during the recent
campaign.


He stated that the Labor Journal,
as the official organ of the council,
had no right to support Boyd. Edi-
tor Harbaugh responded warmly to
Gilbert's criticisms. A committee
was appointed by the council to in
vestigate the course of the paper dur
ing the campaign.


The matter will be dropped in all
probability, as labor leaders in gen
eral are not disposed to criticize the
Labor Journal for it's course during
the campaign.


Reports were made at the meeting
upon the signing OF the stablemen's
and brewery workers scales. No re-
port was presented to the council
upon any of the existing labor
troubles.
